when-you-transfer-data-outside-the-eu: When you transfer data outside the European Union:
check-whether-country-has-data-protectio: Check whether the country outside the EU to which you are transferring the data has data protection legislation and whether it is recognised as adequate by the European Commission.
a-map-of-the-world-presenting-data-prote: A map of the world presenting data protection legislation.
otherwise-provide-legal-framework-for-tr: Otherwise, you will have to provide a legal framework for your transfers to ensure data protection abroad.
please-attach-relevant-documents-to-the-: Please attach relevant documents to the task.
ensure-integrity-minimize-risk: Ensure the integrity of your data assets by minimizing the risk of data loss or hacking.
measures-depend-on-sensitiveness-and-ris: The measures to be taken, whether electronic or physical, depend on the sensitiveness of the data you are processing and the risks to data subjects in the event of an incident.
various-actions-antivirus-passwords-encr: Various actions must be implemented: updating your antivirus and software, regularly changing passwords and adopting complex passwords, or encrypting your data in certain situations. In the event of loss or theft of an electronic device, it will be more difficult for a third party to access it.
please-attach-security-certification-doc: Please attach the relevant security certification and documents to the task.
list-all-persons-who-have-access-to-the-: List all persons who have access to the data;
for-example-recruitment-it-management-pr: For example: recruitment department, IT department, management, service providers, partners, hosts, etc.
processing-must-have-purpose: A data processing operation must have a purpose, a finality, i.e. you cannot collect or process personal data simply in case it would be useful to you one day. Each data processing operation must be assigned a purpose, which must of course be lawful and legitimate in the context of your professional activity.
processing-purpose-example: Example: You collect a lot of information from your customers, when you make a delivery, issue an invoice or offer a loyalty card. All these operations on these data represent your processing of personal data for the purpose of managing your customers.
personal-data-processing-definition: A personal data processing is an operation, or set of operations, involving personal data, whatever the method used (collection, recording, organisation, storage, adaptation, modification, extraction, consultation, use, communication by diffusion, transmission or any other form of making available, linkage).

unknown.label: Unknown
unknown.description: Has not even been checked yet

not-performed.label: Not Performed
not-performed.description: Complete lack of recognizable policy, procedure, control etc.

performed-informally.label: Performed Informally
performed-informally.description: Development has barely started and will require significant work to fulfill the requirements

planned.label: Planned
planned.description: Progressing nicely but not yet complete

well-defined.label: Well Defined
well-defined.description: Development is more or less complete, although detail is lacking and/or it is not yet implemented, enforced and actively supported by top management

quantitatively-controlled.label: Quantitatively Controlled
quantitatively-controlled.description: Development is complete, the process/control has been implemented and recently started operating

continuously-improving.label: Continuously Improving
continuously-improving.description: The requirement is fully satisfied, is operating fully as expected, is being actively monitored and improved, and there is substantial evidence to prove all that to the auditors

sso.manage-description: Configure SAML and OIDC Single Sign-On connections for this team.
sso.no-connections-description: Create a SAML or OIDC connection to enable Single Sign-On for this team.
sso.create-description: Choose SAML or OIDC and enter the provider metadata.
sso.edit-description: Update provider metadata and connection settings.
sso.raw-idp-xml-description: Paste the raw XML metadata obtained from the SAML provider or enter the metadata URL below.
sso.raw-idp-xml-edit-note: Jackson does not return the original raw XML after saving. Leave the XML and metadata URL empty to keep the current IdP metadata, or enter new metadata to replace it.
sso.raw-idp-xml-edit-placeholder: Leave empty to keep the current IdP metadata
sso.metadata-url-description: URL for the SAML provider metadata.
sso.well-known-url-description: Enter the well-known discovery path of the OpenID provider or manually enter the provider metadata below.
sso.connection-label-placeholder: An internal label to identify the connection
sso.connection-description-placeholder: A short description not more than 100 characters
sso.default-redirect-url-description: URL to redirect the user to after an IdP initiated login.
sso.allowed-redirect-urls-description: URL(s) to redirect the user to after login. Only the URLs in this list are allowed in the OAuth flow.
sso.sort-order-description: Connections are sorted by this value in listing views. Higher values are displayed first.
sso.delete-description: Are you sure you want to delete {{provider}}? This action cannot be undone.
sso.activate-description: This connection will be available for Single Sign-On again.
sso.deactivate-description: Users will not be able to use this connection for Single Sign-On until it is enabled again.
